free_text: The (d,p) reactions below 3.0 MeV.
Department of Physics, Faculty of Science,
Kuwait University.
3 MeV Van de Graaff was used.
Two silicon surface barrier particle detectors
50 mm2 sensitive area were mounted on separate arms
which could be rotated about the target and were set at
radial distances from the target to the detector
surface of 6.0 and 16.0 cm.
The target materials were evaporated on carbon foils.
The thicknesses of all the targets were determined by
the yield of the elastically scattered deuterons from
the target nuclei.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
Journal name corrected (ANP,491,64 -> ADP,491,64).

Na-23 targets were made by evaporating NaBr onto a
carbon foil. The Na-23 areal density was measured to
be about 7 mu-g/cm2.

Figs 5,6
SD: BIB updated.
Self-supporting Al-27 targets were measured to be
approximately 23 mu-g/cm2 and 73 mu-g/cm2 thick which
correspond to an energy loss of about 4 and 14 keV
respectively at a deuteron bombarding energy of 2 MeV.
The Al-27 target made by evaporating Al-27 onto a
carbon foil and had a thickness of about 8 mu-g/cm2
which corresponded to an energy loss of about 2 keV at
a deuteron bombarding energy of 2 MeV.
Figure 7
